title: barringtonia filirachis
intro: Barringtonia filirachis, also known as the 'Sea Poison Tree' or 'Kumis-kumis', is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia, particularly in Malaysia and the Philippines. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its high content of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The health benefits associated with Barringtonia filirachis include its potential in treating various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic potential and effects on human health.
